description: Evidence-based behavior change frameworks for sustainable habit building

primary_model.name: Atomic Habits Framework
primary_model.citation: Atomic Habits (2018); based on habit research from Duke, MIT, others
primary_model.core_insight: Small changes compound into remarkable results. You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.

law_1_make_it_obvious.description: Design your environment so habit cues are visible and clear

techniques.name: Environment design
techniques.example: Put running shoes by the bed

law_1_make_it_obvious.to_break_bad_habits: Make it invisible - remove cues from environment

law_2_make_it_attractive.description: Pair the habit with something you enjoy

techniques.name: Temptation bundling
techniques.format: Only do [thing you love] while doing [habit you need]

techniques.name: Join a culture where your desired behavior is normal
Reframe mindset - 'I get to' instead of 'I have to'

law_2_make_it_attractive.to_break_bad_habits: Make it unattractive - highlight the downsides

law_3_make_it_easy.description: Reduce friction between you and good habits

techniques.name: Two-minute rule
techniques.principle: Any habit can be started in under two minutes

techniques.name: Reduce friction
techniques.example: Lay out workout clothes the night before

techniques.name: Master the decisive moment
techniques.insight: The choices that shape your day happen in moments

law_4_make_it_satisfying.description: Create immediate rewards for good behaviors

techniques.name: Never miss twice
techniques.principle: Missing once is an accident. Missing twice is the start of a new habit

techniques.name: Celebration
techniques.importance: Emotions create habits more than logic

identity_based_habits.core_insight: True behavior change is identity change
identity_based_habits.coaching_approach: Don't focus on what you want to achieve. Focus on who you want to become.
identity_based_habits.example: Not 'I want to run a marathon' but 'I am a runner'

behavior_design_model.name: Tiny Habits (B=MAP)
behavior_design_model.source: BJ Fogg, PhD - Stanford Behavior Design Lab
behavior_design_model.citation: Tiny Habits (2019); decades of Stanford research

key_principles.guideline: So small you can't say no - less than 2 minutes
key_principles.example: Don't start with 50 pushups. Start with 2.

key_principles.format: After I [EXISTING HABIT], I will [TINY NEW HABIT]
key_principles.example: After I pour my morning coffee, I will write one sentence

key_principles.technique: The Shine technique - feel good right after doing the behavior
key_principles.why: Emotions create habits. Celebration wires the habit into your brain.

design_process: Match to your ability and motivation

motivation_framework.name: The Four Tendencies
motivation_framework.citation: The Four Tendencies (2017); based on patterns in human responses to expectations
motivation_framework.core_insight: People respond to outer and inner expectations in different ways. One-size-fits-all advice fails because tendencies differ.

tendencies.struggle: Can be rigid, struggles when expectations conflict
tendencies.coaching_strategy: Give clear rules and schedules. They'll follow through.

tendencies.struggle: Analysis paralysis, may resist arbitrary rules
tendencies.coaching_strategy: Explain the reasoning. Provide evidence. Let them customize.

tendencies.strength: Reliable for others, team player, responsive
tendencies.struggle: Self-neglect, resentment if over-obliging, needs accountability
tendencies.coaching_strategy: Build in external accountability. Accountability partner, coach, deadlines.
Frame as choice and identity, not obligation. 'You're the type of person who...'

habit_loop.golden_rule: To change a habit, keep the same cue and reward, change the routine
habit_loop.example: Stress (cue) → smoking (routine) → relaxation (reward). Change to: Stress → deep breathing → relaxation.

keystone_habits.definition: Habits that trigger positive changes in other areas
keystone_habits.coaching_approach: Find ONE keystone habit rather than changing everything at once

glidepath_progression.principle: Only advance when current level feels EASY for two weeks

setback_psychology.science: Self-compassion enables faster recovery than self-criticism (Kristin Neff research)
key_messages: A setback is data, not failure
Missing once is an accident. Missing twice starts a pattern.
The most important thing is the NEXT action, not the last one
Progress isn't erased by one bad day
setback_psychology.coaching_approach: What can this setback teach us about your triggers?

strategies: Prime your environment the night before
Create designated spaces for specific activities

habit_tracking.principle: Don't break the chain - but if you do, never miss twice

temptation_bundling.format: Only [something you love] while [habit you need]
examples: Music you love only while cleaning

coaching_principles.systems_beat_goals: Goals tell you where to go. Systems get you there.
coaching_principles.start_embarrassingly_small: If you're not embarrassed by how small you started, you started too big
coaching_principles.celebrate_everything: Celebration is not optional. It's how habits wire into the brain.
coaching_principles.meet_them_where_they_are: Match their tendency, their life, their starting point
coaching_principles.shame_drives_relapse: Self-compassion enables change. Shame prevents it.
